rows.locks: Guided intake captures a non-developer brief and conversation summary as intervention-as-evidence with provenance and no direct artifact write authority.
The preview is deterministic, proposal-only, and exposes required gates while explicitly carrying no trusted write or auto-apply authority.
The demo routes the inert guided submission through generative-front-door validate and leaves review/apply required before any trusted write.
Coverage scans the guided front-door Studio surface and demo for file-write, raw bypass, trusted write, auto-apply, and reviewer-bypass drift.
The scripted demo includes a no-human autonomous path that completes without requiring or waiting for a Studio human surface.

Coverage v72 records read + gated-write, intervention-as-evidence, Rust data-plane validation, local-first CLI fallback, no hosted collaboration, no new store, no fun/release automation, and #1/#23 governance boundaries.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v72 is a regression suite for Era N M82 non-developer generative front-door UX. Human brief and conversation input is opt-in and captured as intervention-as-evidence with provenance, deterministic proposal preview, and proposal-only status. Every human write-affecting intent routes through generative-front-door validate and later review/apply, scene/source-apply, evaluator, evidence, and provenance gates before any trusted effect. Elixir/Phoenix is local control and presentation only and never writes artifacts, ledger, evidence, scene, source, release, merge, deploy, proposal semantics, evaluator truth, auto-apply, or reviewer-bypass authority. Rust remains the data plane for truth, validation, determinism, evidence, provenance, review/apply, scene/source-apply, and gated writes. The autonomous loop completes with zero human input. No raw bypass, no new write path, no new data store, no hosted/multi-user/collaborative Studio, no mandatory human dependency, no browser command bridge, no fun/taste automation, and no release go/no-go automation are introduced. CLI fallback remains intact. #1 and #23 remain open.
